2.speaker: Crab
2.text: Oh, nothing really. Just watching the waves as usual.

3.speaker: Crab
3.text: It's gotten quite dark has it not?

4.speaker: You
4.text: Yeah. We spent all day talking, haha.

8.speaker: You
8.text: I tried my best to help you understand those memories a little better.

9.speaker: Crab
9.text: You did more than that. You helped me look at them through a new lens.

10.speaker: Crab
10.text: So much of the world has been destroyed and rebuilt. Over and over.

11.speaker: Crab
11.text: And yet, look at me. I am still the same. My shell has not shifted. I will still crawl along this shore just as my ancestors did.

12.speaker: Crab
12.text: But... I used to think that meant my existence was empty. That I was just a clock ticking away in the sand.

14.speaker: Crab
14.text: Now, these memories no longer feel like such a burden.

17.speaker: Crab
17.text: Because of the giants. The 'dinosaurs'. Their time was brief, but it meant everything to them.

18.speaker: Crab
18.text: I realize now... it is not how much time you have that gives it meaning.

19.speaker: You
19.text: And for your kind, it's a very long time.

21.speaker: Crab
21.text: The people in the churches. Or the fallen in the wars. Most of their names are entirely forgotten.

22.speaker: Crab
22.text: But they still left something, even if it's not written. It was enough to give themselves meaning.

23.speaker: Crab
23.text: I thought evolution meant growing new claws. A physical transformation to prove I had changed.

24.speaker: Crab
24.text: But your modern world... showed me that isn't needed in order to change.

25.speaker: Crab
25.text: You just need a new perspective.

26.speaker: You
26.text: That's all it really is, isn't it?

28.speaker: Crab
28.text: The memories inside me... they always felt so heavy, yet empty.

30.speaker: Crab
30.text: But by telling them to you... and having you listen... they became more than just memories... more than time itself.

31.speaker: Crab
31.text: They became stories. A part of myself.

32.speaker: You
32.text: That's what stories are for. They're not just for the listener. But the teller as well.

37.speaker: Crab
37.text: I am not sure what to call it.

38.speaker: You
38.text: Maybe that's the feeling of change.

40.speaker: Crab
40.text: The tides are still the same.

41.speaker: Crab
41.text: Same sound. Same pull. Same shore.

42.speaker: Crab
42.text: But I think I am hearing them differently tonight.

48.speaker: Crab
48.text: Perhaps I will count how many waves crash tonight.

51.text: *The waves drift slowly, forward and back*

52.text: *And a faint feeling of something new, stirs in the air*
